entries: The Guild Wars franchise is the flagship franchise of the Washington state based development studio ArenaNet.
If first entry into it's now 20 year old story is Guild Wars Prophecies, as it was later known.
This book aims to capture the story and the feelings of this beloved game, and bring them to the gaming table with the help of the Dungeons & Dragons 5th edition rule set.
All credit goes to the amazing people at ArenaNet and NCSoft, who have given us players such magical adventures.

entries.name: General Information
entries: The following elements are the same for all adventures across all Guild Wars campaigns:

items.name: Player Count
entry: The adventures are designed for any number of players, encounters and treasure tables are structured in a way that they scale with the number of players. 'N' is the number of players in the party. Encounter and treasure tables are cumulative, meaning if the party has 'N' players, all the entries up to 'N' players are used.

At the beginning of each adventure is an overview of the recommended skill proficiencies and spells the party should have access to. If the party does not have access to the recommended proficiencies and spells, consider adjusting the adventure to fit the party's capabilities.

entries.name: The Underworld
entries: The Underworld is the places human souls arrive at after dying on Tyria. There {@deity Grenth|GW} is supposed to Judge them and send them to the afterlife they deserve. This usually means the souls move on to one of the other god realms.

In the past 30 odd years this has no longer been the case. {@deity Dhuum|GW}, defeated long ago by Grenth has broken free of his imprisonment in the Hall of Judgement. Now his reign of terror continues where it has left off more than a thousand years ago.

While Dhuum rules the underworld, resurrections are impossible if the soul has entered the Underworld, which happens one minute after death. To bring back potential allies or defeated party members which where not resurrect quickly after death, players need to venture into the Underworld. There they can, if they are below level 12, release single souls by freeing them from the {@creature Eater of Souls|GW}, a terrifying Undead in the service of Dhuum. Alternatively, they can, if they are strong enough, attempt to free the Underworld from Dhuum's influence by first freeing the {@creature Reaper of Grenth|GW|Seven Reapers} and then confronting the God of Death himself. If the players are successful, all resurrections will be possible again.

{@italic When playing the campaigns Prophecies, Factions, Nightfall and Eye of the North in order, defeating Dhuum will have consequences on the following campaigns. It is not intended to defeat Dhuum once in each campaign. If Dhuum never gets defeated and you plan on playing the Guild Wars 2 campaign and its sequels consider if in the 250 year between the games another adventuring party might have defeated him, otherwise you can run the Hall of Chains adventure in Guild Wars 2.}

entries.name: Travel to the Underworld
entries: Characters who travel into the Underworld who are not Ascended or Whe no Su suffer major penalties if they stay for too long. At the end of each {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} they must make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or gain one level of {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB}, that cannot be removed by long resting as long as they remain in the Underworld.

Traveling from Tyria to the Underworld is no trivial affair. For low level character consider giving them access via a one time use Item or a friendly NPC. The locations most appropriate to travel from are the Temple of the Ages in Kryta, the Temple to Grenth in the Shiverpeaks, The Catacombs in Ascalon, Zin Ku Corridor in Kaineng City or the Chantry of Secrets in Kourna. For higher level characters the Material component for the {@spell Plane Shift} spell is not a common item, finding one can be an adventure itself. The Material component for the return trip is considerably easier to find. Most major cities have NPCs which could sell them, like High Priests or powerful arcane mages like {@creature Magi Malaquire|GWP} or {@creature Vizier Khilbron|GWP}.

entries.name: The Regions of the Underworld
entries: The Underworld is divided into several regions, each once ruled by a {@creature Reaper of Grenth|GW}, now imprisoned by servants of {@deity Dhuum|GW}. The regions are:

items.name: Hall of Judgement
entries: Home of the ruler of the Underworld. Currently occupied by {@deity Dhuum|GW}. Can only be accessed once all seven {@creature Reaper of Grenth|GW} have been freed.

items.name: The Labyrinth
entries: Arrival destination for all mortals who dare travel to the Underworld. Guarded by the {@creature Minotaur of Dhuum|GW}. The {@creature Reaper of Grenth|GW|Reaper of the Labyrinth} is imprisoned here.

items.name: Icy Wastes
entries: A large stretch of frozen land, home to a huge sentient {@creature Ice Storm|GW}. The {@creature Reaper of Grenth|GW|Reaper of the Icy Wastes} is imprisoned here.

items.name: Bone Pits
entries: A vast graveyard filled with the bones of the dead tended to by the {@creature Eater of Souls|GW}. The {@creature Reaper of Grenth|GW|Reaper of the Bone Pits} is imprisoned here.

items.name: Twin Serpent Mountains
entries: A treacherous mountain range filled with twisting paths and deadly creatures. Two gargantuan {@creature Serpent of Entropy|GW|Serpents of Entropy} inhabit these peaks. The {@creature Reaper of Grenth|GW|Reaper of the Twin Serpent Mountains} is imprisoned here.

items.name: Forgotten Vale
entries: A hidden valley shrouded in mist and mystery. Visions of the past haunt those who enter. The {@creature Reaper of Grenth|GW|Reaper of the Forgotten Vale} is imprisoned here.

items.name: Spawning Pools
entries: A murky and dangerous area filled with dark waters and strange creatures. The {@creature Reaper of Grenth|GW|Reaper of the Spawning Pools} is imprisoned here.

items.name: Chaos Planes
entries: A realm of pure chaos and destruction. The {@creature Reaper of Grenth|GW|Reaper of the Chaos Planes} is imprisoned here.

entries.name: The Fissure of Woe
entries: The Fissure of Woe is the realm of the God of War and Fire Balthazar. True to its god's domains, it is a place of constant strife as souls who end up in this afterlife are embroiled in an endless war against the forces of Menzies, Balthazar's evil half brother.

Characters who end up in the Fissure of Woe can help the {@creature Eternal Forgemaster|GW} retake his Forge in the {@area Temple of War||x} to use it to craft powerful armaments. Other Eternals throughout the fissure can also be helped to gain Balthazar's favour.

entries.name: Travel to the Fissure of Woe
entries: Characters who travel into the Fissure of Woe who are not Ascended or Whe no Su suffer major penalties if they stay for too long. At the end of each {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} they must make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or gain one level of {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB}, that cannot be removed by long resting as long as they remain in the Fissure of Woe.

Traveling from Tyria to the Fissure of Woe is no trivial affair. For low level character consider giving them access via a one time use Item or a friendly NPC. The locations most appropriate to travel from are the Temple of the Ages in Kryta, the Temple to Balthazar in the Maguuma Jungle, Zin Ku Corridor in Kaineng City or the Chantry of Secrets in Kourna. For higher level characters the Material component for the {@spell Plane Shift} spell is not a common item, finding one can be an adventure itself. The Material component for the return trip is considerably easier to find. Most major cities have NPCs which could sell them, like High Priests or powerful arcane mages like {@creature Magi Malaquire|GWP} or {@creature Vizier Khilbron|GWP}.

entries.name: The Regions of the Fissure of Woe
entries: The Fissure of Woe is divided into several regions, each having their own conflict between the Eternals and the Shadow Army. The regions are:

items.name: Burning Forest
entries: The Burning Forest is an immolated forest located in the north-eastern corner of the Fissure of Woe. There is a great fire in the center of the forest, as well as numerous lava-emitting spores. There is no green vegetation in this forest whatsoever. The forest is harvested by the Shadow army, followers of Menzies, for weapon materials. The Burning Forest can be accessed from the Tower of Strength or from the Great Battle Field.
{@b Slaves of Menzies:} {@creature Eternal Warrior|GW|Miko the Unchained} wants the characters to free the slaves the Shadow Army uses to harvest the wood.

items.name: Fissure Shore
entries: The Fissure Shore, also known as the Fissure Beach, is the coastal region located in the south-eastern section of the Fissure of Woe. There are a number of shipwrecks scattered down the length of the Fissure Shore. The shore is connected to both the Spider Cave and the Tower of Courage.

items.name: Forest of the Wailing Lord
entries: The Forest of the Wailing Lord is a small forest located in the north-western section of the Fissure of Woe. The forest is governed by the {@creature Wailing Lord|GW}, who allows both the Eternal army and the Shadow army to harvest the forest to fuel their war efforts. The forest can be reached by traveling north-west from the Tower of Courage, or by traveling to the west of the northern part of the Great Battle Field.
{@b A Gift of Griffons:} The Wailing Lord wishes to gift Balthazar three {@creature Forest Griffon|GW|Forest Griffons}, the character are tasked with herding them to the Temple of War.

items.name: Great Battle Field
entries: The Great Battle Field is a large area of plains sweeping to the north of the Temple of War in the Fissure of Woe. It is the site of the never-ending battle between Balthazar's eternal army and Menzies' shadow army. The field contains two battle camps, one for each opposing army. There are many exits from the great battle field: north-east to the Burning Forest, south to the Temple of War, south-west to the Tower of Courage, or north-west to the Forest of the Wailing Lord.
The field is patrolled largely by the skeletal army, an army comprised of the fallen soldiers of both the shadow army and the eternal army.

{@b Army of Darkness:} {@creature Eternal Warrior|GW|Kromril the Eternal} wants the characters to find out what new dark sorcery the Shadow Army is using. They are tasked with assassinating the {@creature Priest of Menzies|GW} an retrieving any unholy texts they have.

{@b The Eternal Forgemaster:} The {@creature Eternal Monk|GW|Eternal Weaponsmith} wants the characters to head to the Temple of War, clear out the Shadow Army occupying it and free the {@creature Eternal Forgemaster|GW} that that he may forge them armaments.

items.name: Lake of Fire
entries: The Lake of Fire is an area of the Fissure of Woe located in the south-eastern corner. The area is typified by large lava pools flowing from a giant volcano at the edge of the area. There is only one exit from the lake, which leads back towards the Tower of Strength or the Temple of War.

items.name: Spider Cave
entries: The Spider Cave is located to the south-east of the Temple of War and forms the tunnel that feeds the forge inside the temple. The cave connects the Temple of War to the Fissure Shore.
{@b The Hunt:} {@creature Nimros the Hunter|GW} wants the characters to hunt down a tag seven {@creature Shard Wolf|GW|Shard Wolves} throughout the Fissure of Woe.

items.name: Temple of War
entries: The Temple of War (also known as The Forge) is a mighty fortress of the Eternals found in the center of the Fissure of Woe. The temple has a north and a south entrance. Both of these entrances are locked until the groups of Shadow army guarding them are defeated.
{@b Defend the Temple of War:} The Eternal Forgemaster asks the characters to help defend the Temple of War until backup arrives.

{@b Khobay the Betrayer:} The Temple of War didn't fall in a fair fight, Khobay an Eternal betrayed Balthazar, now that the Temple is safe again, the Eternal Forgemaster wants the characters to go out into the Lake of Fire to bring the betrayers to justice.

{@b Restore the Temple of War:} In the short time the Temple of War was occupied by the Shadow army, they corrupted its divine power. The Eternal Forgemaster needs the characters to cleanse the Seeds of corruption that have been planted in the surrounding regions.

{@b Tower of Strength:} {@creature Eternal Warrior|GW|Eternal Lord Taeres} is ready to strike at the heart of Menzies power in the Fissure of Woe, the Tower of Strength and wants the characters to join the assault.

items.name: Tower of Courage
entries: The Tower of Courage is a large tower in the Fissure of Woe located west of the Temple of War. The tower has three turrets and an initially locked entrance. From the tower, characters can travel east to the Great Battle Field or south to the Fissure Shore.
{@b Tower of Courage:} {@creature Eternal Warrior|GW|Rastigan the Eternal} needs the characters to reclaim the Tower of Courage in the name of Balthazar.

{@b The Wailing Lord:} {@creature Eternal Warrior|GW|Rastigan the Eternal} wants the characters to go into the Forest of the Wailing Lord and destroy his {@creature Banshee|GW|Banshees} to teach him a lesson.

items.name: Tower of Strength
entries: The Tower of Strength is a large tower in the Fissure of Woe located directly to the east of the Temple of War. Similar to the Tower of Courage, the Tower of Strength has three turrets and an initially locked entrance. From the tower, characters can travel northwest to the Great Battle Field, south to the Lake of Fire, or north to the Burning Forest.

entries.name: Fissure of Woe General Features
entries: {@b Light.} The Fissure of Woe is under perpetual cloud cover as multiple active volcanos spew forth ash and smoke. It is unclear if there is a sun above the clouds, as there are no changes in brightness over time.

{@b Atmosphere.} The air in the Fissure of Woe is thick with ash, smoke and the iron smell of blood. While it is not difficult to breath, spending much time here is unpleasant for creatures relying on air.

{@b Terrain.} Fauna and Flora in the Fissure of Woe are exceedingly rare, most inhabitants are spirits and the undead. The ground is often craggy and dry, as rain is basically non-existent. Muted browns and gray dominated the landscape. Scars of ancient battles can be found everywhere, broken weapons, discarded armor and half buried skeletons are as common as trees on Tyria.

{@b Travel.} Travel in the fissure is a dangerous thing to do, as there are man forces who will attempt to waylay travelers. See Appendix A: Random encounters on how to handle travel in the Fissure of Woe.

entries.name: Arrival in the Fissure of Woe
entries: The characters can arrive in any location detailed above, but the two locations that make the most sense are the Great Battle Field and the Tower of Courage. There they encounter quickly either the {@creature Eternal Monk|GW|Eternal Weaponsmith} or {@creature Eternal Warrior|GW|Rastigan the Eternal} respectively. Either one can give the characters an introduction into the Fissure of Woe and ask them for aid or send them to wherever you want the characters to go.
While there are specific adventures the characters can have in the Fissure of Woe, as detailed below, there is more than enough space to add or remove to make it fit into your campaign.

entries.name: Features
entries: The Great Battle Field is the main place of conflict for the war between Balthazar's Forces, the Eternals, and his evil half-brother's, Menzies, forces, the Shadow army. This conflict has raged on longer than anyone can remember, and the battlefield can attest to that. The ground is made up of countless generations of fallen soldiers and their armaments, having been ground to dust by the armies marching over them.

Strangest of all are groups of skeletal soldiers patrolling the battlefield. These soldiers are the fallen of both armies, hostile to both armies, having been reanimated by an unknown source. The smartest strategist of both sides regularly use them to their advantage.

While the battle field is by far the largest and most frequent used place of conflict between the opposing forces, there are also plenty of smaller skirmishes in every region of the fissure. The battle field connects to the {@area Burning Forest|GW7b5|x} to the north-east, to the {@area Temple of War|GW7b11|x} to the south, to the {@area Tower of Courage|GW7b12|x} to the south-west and to the {@area Forest of the Wailing Lord|GW7b7|x} to the north-west.

entries.name: Important Locations and NPCs
entries: The two most important locations are the two war camps of the opposing armies. The warcamp of the Eternals is located to the south-west, close to the Forest of the Wailing Lord, while the warcamp of the Shadow army is in the north-east, close to the Burning Forest. Both camps are heavily fortified and are the main source of reinforcements for their respective armies.

Stationed in the Eternals warcamp are two NPCs of particular interest to the characters, {@creature Eternal Warrior|GW|Kromril the Eternal} and the {@creature Eternal Monk|GW|Eternal Weaponsmith}. Kromril is a powerful commander of the Eternals, and is always looking for new ways to gain an advantage over the Shadow army. The Eternal Weaponsmith is a master craftsman who can forge powerful weapons and armor for the characters if they help him out with some tasks around the Fissure of Woe. See their respective quests below.

entries.name: The Eternal Forgemaster: Part I

entries: As you make your war through the warcamp, accompanied by the ever present noise of an army the size of a small city, you come across a haphazardly built forge, with a single spectral figure working at it. He is clad in ornate plate armor, with an intricate dragon design. In his spectral features you can make out the expression of a master craftsman deeply upset about the state of his forge. He looks up at you and says:

"Mortal, aren't you? You must be some sort of hero to be welcomed into the domain of the God of War. I could try to improve your armaments with the forge I have right here, but it would be a poor substitute of what the {@creature Eternal Forgemaster|GW} and I can accomplish when using the Forge at the {@area Temple of War|GW7b9|x}!"

The Eternal Weaponsmith then explains that the Temple of War has recently fallen into the hands of the Shadow army, after the Eternals were betrayed by one of their own. The Eternal Forgemaster was captured by the enemy and is now forced to construct armaments for the enemy. Scouts report that apparently only a few soldiers and a general were left behind to defend the Temple of War, as the Shadow army believes the innate defenses of the temple will be enough to deter the Eternals. This thought was true until the characters arrived, as the defenses of the temple are dedicated to keeping spirits and undead out.

The Eternal Weaponsmith then asks the characters to go to the Temple of War, infiltrate it, defeat the Shadow army silently, as not to alert the rest of the forces, and to free the Eternal Forgemaster. He hands them a map of the fissure and points to a volcano on the southern horizon and explains that the temple is built atop the volcano, and that the only way in are the two gates, as the caldera is protected by a shield of fire, preventing arial incursions. Each gate can only be opened from the inside with a lever next to it. Since the gates are designed to keep out only the denizens of the fissure, the characters should be able to bypass them by conventional means.

He accompanies them, helping in combat if you want him to, and can hand them a {@item Spell Scroll (Level 6)|XDMG|Spell Scroll} of {@spell Arcane Gate|XPHB} if they ask for help getting inside. The travel to the foot of the volcano takes 4 hours and happens exclusively in the Great Battle Field.
{@i This Adventure continues in the {@area Temple of War|GW7b11|x} section.

entries.name: Features
entries: The Temple of War is the holiest place in the Fissure of Woe, it not only houses the Forge, where the Eternals source their armaments, but it also serves as the main base of operations for the Eternals. While Balthazar was still roaming Tyria this was his preferred place of residence. It is located atop of the largest and most active volcano in the fissure, many miles in the sky, overlooking the entire realm.

The temple is heavily fortified, with powerful magical defenses designed to keep out spirits and the undead. The temple is currently occupied by the Shadow army, after the Eternal Khobay betrayed his own. They banished or killed all but one Eternal, the Eternal Forgemaster, who is now forced to craft corrupted armaments for them.

entries.name: Important Locations and NPCs
entries: Located at the very top of the volcano, encompassing the entire caldera is the Forge. It is run by the Eternal Forgemaster, a powerful Eternal who is the most skilled craftsman in the Fissure of Woe. The magma spewing forth is harnessed to power the Forge, imbuing the armaments created there with the destructive force of the heart of a volcano.

Crisscrossing the entire volcano are many passages who connect the various subterranean quarters of the temple. The most important of these quarters is the divine quarter. This is where Balthazar used to reside, and his section is a strictly prohibited zone for even the highest ranking eternals. Not even the occupying force of the Shadow army has dared set foot in there, for fear of incurring Balthazar's wrath.

entries.name: The Eternal Forgemaster: Part II
entries: {@i This is a continuation of the adventure started in the {@area Great Battle Field|GW7b8|x} section.}

As you approach the foot of the massive volcano you first realize really how massive it really is. Jutting out of the otherwise flat landscape, the volcano towers roughly 3 miles into the dark and murky sky. A long and treacherous climb is ahead of you.
If the characters do not use magical means to climb the volcano it takes roughly a full day of climbing to get up.

A large sulfurs cloud passes over you, and as the peak clears you can finally see the Forge for the first time. The entire caldera is ringed in massive metal spikes crowning a circular construction of metal and stone in the middle. Hovering above the lava, connected by four steel bridges to an outer ring carved into the mountain, is a large metal platform hanging from chains that themselves are connected to large pulleys on the outer ring.
The entrance to the Forge is a large wrought iron gate the size of a house. Looking through the metal bars you can see a group of Shadow army soldiers guarding the only lever that can open the gate.

Since the temple is designed to keep out spirits and the undead none of the wards prevent mortals from crossing its boundaries by magical or mundane means. Teleporting through the gate is the easiest method of getting in, but it can also be destroyed (HP: 100; AC: 15; {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to Psychic and Poison damage, {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Bludgeoning, Piercing and Slashing damage from non-magical sources). Operating the level requires a {@action Utilize|XPHB} action, which causes the gate to start opening, fully opening at the end of the next round. Guarding the gate is a {@table Determine Your XP Budget; XP Budget per Character|XDMG|Low Difficulty Encounter} using monsters of your choice from the Shadow army. If the characters do not use loud spells or abilities they might be able to take them out silently. At least one hostile combatant will try to run away and alert the other members of the Shadow army in the Forge, which include a similar strength group at the other gate opposite the one the characters are at, and the {@creature Abyssal|GW|commander} of the forces, who is at the heart of the forge, forcing the Eternal Forgemaster to work.
Once all the forces are defeated the Eternal Forgemaster can be freed.

You see in center of the platform, next to a massive adamantine Anvil, is a spectral Centaur the Eternal Forgemaster, he looks up to you and says: "You have helped rid the Temple of War of the vile servants of Menzies. For this do I deem you worthy to receive armaments crafted at the Forge. Before I can do so it must be cleansed of the vile corruption affecting it."

{@i {@b Reward.} For freeing the temple and the forgemaster the characters receive {@variantrule Experience Points|XPHB} equal to a {@table Determine Your XP Budget; XP Budget per Character|XDMG|High Difficulty Encounter} and rare materials used to craft armaments in the forge with a value of 4000 GP.}

entries.name: Restore the Temple of War
entries: {@i This adventure can only be started after The Eternal Forgemaster adventure has concluded.}

The Eternal Forgemaster approaches you: "Here at the heart of the Temple of War are forged the very arms and armor of the gods. This most sacred of places has been defiled. During the brief period while the vile Shadow Army of Menzies usurped the Temple, they choked the pure fires of the forge with a vile miasma used to imbue all their weapons with an unholy taint."
"Though they have been driven from the Temple, the source of the corruption remains in the tunnels that feed the forge. Go to the tunnels and cleanse all seeds of corruption."

Feeding the forge are multiple tunnels of magma, in these tunnels are Seeds of Corruption, crystalline entities who where once tasked with imbuing the molten rock with divine energies. Now corrupted they taint the magma used to forge. The Eternal Weaponsmith can bless the characters with fire {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} for 1 hour, allowing them to swim down the tunnels. While submerged in magma the characters are {@condition Blinded|XPHB} and {@condition Deafened|XPHB} and are {@hazard Suffocation|XPHB|Suffocating}.

To find a seed the characters must {@action Search|XPHB} the magma tunnels. For every minute of searching they can attempt a {@dc 20} {@skill Perception} check with {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} if they do not have any senses mitigating the blindness and deafness. Once they locate a seed they can cleanse it either with a {@dc 20} {@skill Religion} check, that takes one minute to complete, or a {@spell Greater Restoration|XPHB} spell or spell of greater power that can cleanse corruption or remove curses. There are as many seeds as there are characters. Once all the seed are cleansed and the characters return to the Eternal Forgemaster he tells them:
"I shall bear witness of your deeds to Balthazar. The fires burn pure once more. Perhaps now I can resume my work."
The Eternal Forgemaster can now use the Forge to enhance the armaments of the characters using materials native to the Fissure of Woe and other powerful materials. See the Forge Crafting sidebar.

{@i {@b Reward.} For cleansing the Seeds of Corruption the characters receive {@variantrule Experience Points|XPHB} equal to a {@table Determine Your XP Budget; XP Budget per Character|XDMG|Medium Difficulty Encounter} and receive each one {@item Obsidian Shard|GW}. The character also receive the material component needed for the {@spell Plane Shift} spell, both for the material plane, an the Fissure of Woe.}

entries.name: Forge Crafting
entries: A large part of using the Forge is channeling ones own spirit and dedication into the armaments crafted for them. While the Eternal Forgemaster and Eternal Weaponmaster do the actual forging, the character guides the process and is instrumental in the success of the forging process.

For characters trying to upgrade one of their Guild Wars 2 Legendary precursor items consult the table below for the DCs. If they fail a check they loose the materials invested up to that point and can re-attempt the upgrade, as long as they have the required materials. Appropriate checks for the characters are any tool associated with the item they are trying to upgrade, their spellcasting ability or one ability score associated with how they use the item.

entries.name: Mystic Forge Crafting DCs for Legendary Precursor Upgrades

entries: Other guaranteed crafts can be found on the table below.

entries.name: Defend the Temple of War
entries: {@i This adventure can only be started after Restore the Temple of War adventure has concluded.}

The Eternal Forgemaster approaches you: "Menzies, Lord of Destruction, will not rest while we hold the Temple of War. There can be no doubt he will launch a new attack very soon. Balthazar will send new Temple Guards shortly. In the meantime, will you remain and fight the next wave of Shadow Army Invaders?"
Cleansing the Seeds of Corruption has alerted Menzies, who now send a large force of Shadow army soldiers, trying to retake it before the defenses can reactivate.

entries.name: Overview
entries: This appendix contains a list of random encounters that can occur while traveling in the world of Guild Wars. These encounters can be used to add variety and excitement to your game, and can be tailored to fit the specific needs of your campaign.

The encounters are divided by region, and each region has two different tables: one for terrain encounters and one for creature encounters. To use the tables, roll a dice depending on the region to determine after how long an encounter occurs, then roll on the terrain and creature encounter tables to create the encounter.

Each encounter includes a brief description, the creatures involved, and any relevant statistics or mechanics. The encounters can be adjusted in difficulty based on the level of the player characters, and can be modified to fit the specific needs of your campaign.

entries.name: Arial
entries: Duration between Encounters: {@dice 2d4} hours.

entries.name: Burning Forest
entries: Duration between Encounters: {@dice 1d6} hours.

entries.name: Fissure Shore
entries: Duration between Encounters: {@dice 1d8} hours.

entries.name: Forest of the Wailing Lord
entries: Duration between Encounters: {@dice 1d6} hours.

data.name: Appendix C: Spells
entries: List of all Spells.

data.name: Glossary
entries: List of all important Things.

item.name: Quiver of Elona
entries: This quiver is made from the finest materials and is enchanted to hold many different types of ammunition. It is said to have been crafted by the greatest archers of Elona, and is a prized possession among those who rely on ranged combat.

Each of the quiver's ten compartments connects to an extra dimensional space that allows the quiver to hold numerous items while never weighing more than 2 pounds. Each compartment can hold up to 100 pieces of {@table ammunition|XPHB}.
You can draw any item the quiver contains as if doing so from a regular quiver or scabbard.

item.name: Titanstring Longbow
entries: Made from the strings of slain titans, this special ranged weapon rewards brute strength over dexterity.
Attack and damage rolls made with this weapon use Strength instead of Dexterity.

item.name: Antimagic Shackles
entries: These shackles are imbued with powerful anti-magic wards.

You can take a {@action Utilize|XPHB} action to place these shackles on a creature that has the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition. The shackles adjust to fit a creature of Small to Large size. The shackles prevent the creature from casting spells, using the {@action Magic|XPHB} action, or creating other magical effects.

You and any creature you designate when you use the shackles can take a {@action Utilize|XPHB} action to remove them. Once every day, the bound creature can make a DC 25 Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) check. On a successful check, the creature breaks free and destroys the shackles.

item.name: Tome of Capture
entries: This special Tome was crafted by Magi Malaquire. On the first page it urges you to 'Capture them all', whatever that means.
As a reaction, when a hostile enemy within 150 feet reaches 0 hit points, or as a magic action, you can cast the {@spell Signet of Capture|GW} spell using this tome.
When doing so, you can capture an unlimited number of spells in one casting of {@spell Signet of Capture|GW}, but instead of learning them, they are recorded in this tome.
Certain powerful trainers can record spells in this book by spending 1 minute in study.

Any creature can learn a spell recorded in this tome, if it is on the creatures spell list, by spending 10 minutes in study with the tome per spell level. When doing so, the creature can replace a currently prepared spell with the spell learned from this tome.
A spell can be learned from this tome an unlimited number of times by any creature holding the tome or a bonded {@item Signet Ring of Signet of Capture|GW}.

item.name: Signet Ring of Signet of Capture
entries: This special Ring was crafted by Magi Malaquire.
As a reaction, when a hostile enemy within 150 feet reaches 0 hit points, or as a magic action, you can cast the {@spell Signet of Capture|GW} spell using this ring.

When doing so, you can capture an unlimited number of spells in one casting of {@spell Signet of Capture|GW}, but instead of learning them, they are recorded in the {@item Tome of Capture|GW} this ring is bonded to.
As a magic action you can bond this ring to a {@item Tome of Capture|GW}.

item.name: Potion of Arcane Recovery
entries: The potion's blue liquid crackles with arcane energy.
You can choose expended spell slots to recover. The spell slots can have a combined level equal to {@dice 1d4}.
If you have the Pact Magic feature you instead regain 1 spell slot.

magicvariant.name: Rope Ammunition (Common)
entries: Rope Ammunition places a magical rope at the location the ammunition hits.

Instead of attacking normally with this ammunition, you can choose to conjure a 50 foot long rope that extends from the point of impact to a point within 50 feet of it. The rope is anchored at both ends and can support up to 500 pounds. Once it lands, the ammunition is no longer magical.

This ammunition is typically found or sold in quantities of ten or twenty pieces. Ten pieces of this ammunition are equivalent in value to a potion of the same rarity ({@table Magic Item Values by Rarity; Magic Item Rarities and Values|XDMG|50 GP}).

magicvariant.name: Illuminating Ammunition (Common)
entries: Illuminating Ammunition creates a bright light at the location the ammunition hits.

Instead of attacking normally with this ammunition, you can choose to conjure a magical light that sheds {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 20-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 20 feet around the point of impact. The light lasts for 1 minute and can be colored as you like. Once it lands, the ammunition is no longer magical.

This ammunition is typically found or sold in quantities of ten or twenty pieces. Ten pieces of this ammunition are equivalent in value to a potion of the same rarity ({@table Magic Item Values by Rarity; Magic Item Rarities and Values|XDMG|50 GP}).

magicvariant.name: Elemental Ammunition (Common)
entries: Elemental Ammunition comes in six varieties: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Poison, and Thunder. Each piece of ammunition is imbued with the power of one of the six elements.

Your damage rolls made with this magic ammunition deal the damage type of the element it is imbued with instead of its normal damage type and an additional {@damage 1d6} damage of that type. Once it hits a target, the ammunition is no longer magical.

This ammunition is typically found or sold in quantities of five or ten pieces. Five pieces of this ammunition are equivalent in value to a potion of the same rarity ({@table Magic Item Values by Rarity; Magic Item Rarities and Values|XDMG|50 GP}).

magicvariant.name: Ammunition of Transposition (Common)
entries: This ammunition is not meant to be used for attacking, but rather for teleporting. It deals no damage on impact.

Instead of attacking normally with this ammunition, you can choose to teleport to where the ammunition lands, as long as it lands in an unoccupied space within 15 feet of you. Once it lands, the ammunition is no longer magical.

This ammunition is typically found or sold in quantities of five or ten pieces. Five pieces of this ammunition are equivalent in value to a potion of the same rarity ({@table Magic Item Values by Rarity; Magic Item Rarities and Values|XDMG|50 GP}).

magicvariant.name: Ammunition of Dislocation (Common)
entries: This ammunition can magically dislocate a creature from its current location and teleport it somewhere else.

On a hit, the target takes no damage but must succeed on a DC 12 Charisma {@variantrule Saving Throw|XPHB} or be teleported to an unoccupied space of the attacker's choice within 15 feet of the target. Once it hits a target, the ammunition is no longer magical.

This ammunition is typically found or sold in quantities of five or ten pieces. Five pieces of this ammunition are equivalent in value to a potion of the same rarity ({@table Magic Item Values by Rarity; Magic Item Rarities and Values|XDMG|50 GP}).

magicvariant.name: Ammunition of Dislocation (Rare)
entries: This ammunition can magically dislocate a creature from its current location and teleport it somewhere else.

On a hit, the target takes no damage but must succeed on a DC 16 Charisma {@variantrule Saving Throw|XPHB} or be teleported to an unoccupied space of the attacker's choice within 60 feet of the target. Once it hits a target, the ammunition is no longer magical.

This ammunition is typically found or sold in quantities of five or ten pieces. Five pieces of this ammunition are equivalent in value to a potion of the same rarity ({@table Magic Item Values by Rarity; Magic Item Rarities and Values|XDMG|2000 GP}).

magicvariant.name: Ammunition of Many Targets (Common)
entries: This magical ammunition can hit multiple targets.

On a hit, the target suffers the weapon attack's normal effects, and a creature of your choice within 15 feet of the target must make a DC 12 Dexterity {@variantrule Saving Throw|XPHB} or also take the damage the target took from the attack. Once it hits a target, the ammunition is no longer magical.

This ammunition is typically found or sold in quantities of two or four pieces. Two pieces of this ammunition are equivalent in value to a potion of the same rarity ({@table Magic Item Values by Rarity; Magic Item Rarities and Values|XDMG|50 GP}).

magicvariant.name: Ammunition of Smiting (Common)
entries: This magical ammunition smites creatures around the target with a holy beam.

On a hit, each creature in a 5-foot radius, 10-foot high {@variantrule Cylinder [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cylinder} centered on the target must make a DC 12 Constitution {@variantrule Saving Throw|XPHB} or take radiant damage equal to the weapon's damage. Once it hits a target, the ammunition is no longer magical.

This ammunition is typically found or sold in quantities of two or four pieces. Two pieces of this ammunition are equivalent in value to a potion of the same rarity ({@table Magic Item Values by Rarity; Magic Item Rarities and Values|XDMG|50 GP}).

magicvariant.name: Ammunition of Obscuration (Common)
entries: This magical ammunition obscures a target area.

Instead of attacking normally with this ammunition, you can choose to create a cloud of magical smoke where the ammunition lands. The ammunition replicates the effects of a level 1 {@spell Fog Cloud|XPHB} spell (no {@variantrule Concentration|XPHB} required). Once it lands, the ammunition is no longer magical.

This ammunition is typically found or sold in quantities of ten or twenty pieces. Ten pieces of this ammunition are equivalent in value to a potion of the same rarity ({@table Magic Item Values by Rarity; Magic Item Rarities and Values|XDMG|50 GP}).

magicvariant.name: Ammunition of Darkness (Common)
entries: This magical ammunition creates a cloud of darkness.

Instead of attacking normally with this ammunition, you can choose to create a cloud of magical darkness where the ammunition lands. For 1 minute after it lands, the arrow emits a 5-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} of magical {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB} as per the {@spell Darkness|XPHB} spell (no {@variantrule Concentration|XPHB} required). Once it lands, the ammunition is no longer magical.

This ammunition is typically found or sold in quantities of five or ten pieces. Five pieces of this ammunition are equivalent in value to a potion of the same rarity ({@table Magic Item Values by Rarity; Magic Item Rarities and Values|XDMG|50 GP}).

magicvariant.name: Ammunition of Healing (Common)
entries: This ammunition can magically heal a creature.

This ammunition is typically found or sold in quantities of one or two pieces. One piece of this ammunition is equivalent in value to a potion of the same rarity ({@table Magic Item Values by Rarity; Magic Item Rarities and Values|XDMG|50 GP}).

magicvariant.name: Ammunition of Restoration (Common)
entries: This ammunition can magically end a creature's ailments.

On a hit, the target takes no damage but the latest of the following {@variantrule Condition|XPHB|Conditions} is ended: {@condition Blinded|XPHB}, {@condition Deafened|XPHB} or {@condition Poisoned|XPHB}.

This ammunition is typically found or sold in quantities of two or four pieces. Two pieces of this ammunition are equivalent in value to a potion of the same rarity ({@table Magic Item Values by Rarity; Magic Item Rarities and Values|XDMG|50 GP}).

magicvariant.name: Empowered Ammunition (Common)
entries: Empowered Ammunition comes in three varieties: Necrotic, Psychic, and Radiant. Each piece of ammunition is imbued with the power of one of three powers.

Your damage rolls made with this magic ammunition deal the damage type of the power it is imbued with instead of its normal damage type and an additional {@damage 1d4} damage of that type. Once it hits a target, the ammunition is no longer magical.

This ammunition is typically found or sold in quantities of five or ten pieces. Five pieces of this ammunition are equivalent in value to a potion of the same rarity ({@table Magic Item Values by Rarity; Magic Item Rarities and Values|XDMG|50 GP}).

This ammunition is typically found or sold in quantities of five or ten pieces. Five pieces of this ammunition are equivalent in value to a potion of the same rarity ({@table Magic Item Values by Rarity; Magic Item Rarities and Values|XDMG|200 GP}).

magicvariant.name: Explosive Ammunition (Common)
entries: This ammunition explodes on impact.

On a hit, the target takes no damage but each target within 5 feet of it must succeed on a DC 12 Dexterity {@variantrule Saving Throw|XPHB} or take {@damage 2d6} force damage. A creature that succeeds on the saving throw takes half damage. Once it hits a target, the ammunition is no longer magical.

magicvariant.name: Ammunition of Disjunction (Common)
entries: This ammunition disrupts magical effects on the target.

magicvariant.name: Ammunition of Slaying (Common)
entries: This magic ammunition is meant to slay creatures of a particular type, which the DM chooses or determines randomly by rolling on the table below. If a creature of that type takes damage from the ammunition, the creature makes a DC 10 Constitution saving throw, taking an extra {@damage 1d10} Force damage on a failed save or half as much extra damage on a successful one.
After dealing its extra damage to a creature, the ammunition becomes nonmagical.

If a creature of that type takes damage from the ammunition, the creature makes a DC 15 Constitution saving throw, taking an extra {@damage 5d10} Force damage on a failed save or half as much extra damage on a successful one.
After dealing its extra damage to a creature, the ammunition becomes nonmagical.

If a creature of that type takes damage from the ammunition, the creature makes a DC 20 Constitution saving throw, taking an extra {@damage 14d10} Force damage on a failed save or half as much extra damage on a successful one.
After dealing its extra damage to a creature, the ammunition becomes nonmagical.

This magic ammunition is meant to slay creatures of a particular type, which the DM chooses or determines randomly by rolling on the table below. If a creature of that type takes damage from the ammunition, the creature makes a DC 20 Constitution saving throw, taking an extra {@damage 14d10} Force damage on a failed save or half as much extra damage on a successful one.
After dealing its extra damage to a creature, the ammunition becomes nonmagical.

trait.name: Evasion
entries: If Aidan is subjected to an effect that allows him to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, Aidan instead takes no damage if he succeeds on the save and only half damage if he fails. He can't use this trait if he has the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: Aidan makes three attacks, using Scimitar or Longbow in any combination; he can forgo one of these attacks to take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action.

action.name: Scimitar
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 9}, reach 5 ft. {@h}12 ({@damage 2d6 + 5}) Slashing damage plus 14 ({@damage 4d6}) Poison damage.

action.name: Longbow
entries: {@atkr r} {@hit 11}, range 600 ft. {@h}10 ({@damage 1d10 + 7}) Piercing damage plus 14 ({@damage 4d6}) Poison damage.

bonus.name: Deadly Aim
entries: Aidan gives himself {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the next attack roll he makes during the current turn. If that attack hits, the target takes an extra 28 ({@damage 8d6}) Poison damage.
Born in: Kree Foothills
Nation: Ascalon
Profession: Ranger
Age: 32

The son of a huntsman, Aidan grew up with a quiver of arrows on his back. His mother died giving birth to him while his family was on a wagon train from Kryta to Ascalon. As soon as the boy was old enough to hold a bow in one hand, Aidan’s father packed up just the good steel heads of their tools and the two men headed off into the wild. They found a spot in the middle of a forest clearing, refitted their tools with hand-carved handles, and built their own home from scratch.

Aidan is a survivor. Nothing is out of the question if it means he and his companions will live for another day. He thinks fast and acts even faster. His inner calm—a sort of simple confidence—has a way of infecting everyone around him. Being the oldest member of the party, he has the wisdom of years his younger counterparts lack, though he never feels the need to lord it over them. He does feel a certain amount of responsibility to keep the group informed and out of trouble if at all possible. From time to time, he takes on the role of group patriarch, but only if there is a meltdown and he sees the need for his cooler head to prevail.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: Aidan is a 7th-level spellcaster. His spellcasting ability is Wisdom (spell save {@dc 16}, +8 to hit with spell attacks). Aidan has the following Ranger (GW) spells prepared:

entries: Nature Rituals are a type of rituals unique to the ranger profession. These skills summon a spirit that provides a global effect to all those in its area of effect. Unlike Binding Rituals, Nature Rituals affect everyone in their range, enemies and allies alike.
The Nature Ritual spirits resemble miniature druids from the Maguuma Jungle and Melandru's Watcher, the avatar of Melandru, the patron goddess of rangers.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The bone horror makes a number of Swipe attacks equal to half the level of the spell (rounded down) (minimum 1).

action.name: Swipe
entries: Natural Weapon: your spell attack modifier to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h} ({@damage 1d12 + 3} + Spell Level) bludgeoning damage.
Bone Horrors are minions created by certain Necromancer skills (Animate Bone Horror, Malign Intervention, and Aura of the Lich) and the Minion Apocalypse Flux.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The bone fiend makes a number of Spike attacks equal to half the level of the spell (rounded down) (minimum 1).

action.name: Spike
entries: {@atk} your spell attack modifier to hit, range 80/120 ft., one target. {@h} ({@damage 1d8 + 3} + Spell Level) piercing damage.
Bone Horrors are minions created by certain Necromancer skills (Animate Bone Horror, Malign Intervention, and Aura of the Lich) and the Minion Apocalypse Flux.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: Eve casts one of the following spells, using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 15}):

action.name: Multiattack
entries: Eve makes three Arcane Burst attacks.

action.name: Arcane Burst
entries: {@atk ms,rs} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 120 ft., one target. {@h}25 ({@damage 4d10 + 3}) necrotic damage.

bonus.name: Summon Undead (1/Day)
entries: Eve magically summons five {@creature skeleton||skeletons} or {@creature zombie||zombies}. The summoned creatures appear in unoccupied spaces within 60 feet of Eve, whom they obey. They take their turns immediately after Eve. Each lasts for 1 hour, until it or Eve dies, or until Eve dismisses it as a bonus action.

reaction.name: Grim Harvest (1/Turn)
entries: When Eve kills a creature with necrotic damage, she regains 9 ({@damage 2d8}) hit points.
Born in: Unknown
Nation: Ascalon
Profession: Necromancer
Age: 20

Eve hasn't a clue where she was born—she was found as a young child stealing food from a sleeping vagrant in the back alleys of Ascalon City. Fortunately for Eve, she was not found by a city guard but by a kindly matron, who brought her to the Holy Dwayna Academy for Wayward & Incorrigible Girls (Ascalon City branch). Wherever Eve had come from, she had already learned to read at an advanced level before she ended up at the school students called the "Wayward Academy," and voraciously devoured every text in the library. It wasn't long before Eve's curiosity (and her ability to both manipulate and avoid Matron Irma) led her to a secret library in the bowels of the Academy. There, forbidden texts opened up an entirely new world to young Eve. By the time she was 15, she had raised her first bone minion.

Eve had never gotten along with the other girls—she much preferred the company of her tomes and scrolls. When, one day, a few of her fellow students pulled an especially vicious and humiliating prank on her, Eve literally pounced upon the leader of her tormentors, bit the girl’s ear off, and swallowed it whole. That was the end of Eve’s studies at the Holy Dwayna Academy for Wayward & Incorrigible Girls, and not even Matron Irma mourned her departure.

Eve met Mhenlo, Cynn, Devona, and Aidan during the Charr invasion. She was in the graveyard, experimenting with new methods of undead resurrection, when the Charr flooded into the cemetery on the heels of Mhenlo and the others. More out of curiosity than any noble intentions, Eve ordered the Charr to stop. When the beastly creatures turned on her instead, thinking her easy prey, she simply raised her hands and called forth a small army of the dead to meet them. The Charr never stood a chance. Eve was amused when Devona asked the Necromancer to join them, but has since become a good friend of Devona, Aidan, and Mhenlo. She is often in conflict with Cynn, but the two respect each other’s power enough to get along when the going gets rough.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: Nicholas the Traveler makes two attacks, using Shortsword or Longbow in any combination.

action.name: Shortsword
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 5}, reach 5 ft. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d6 + 3}) Piercing damage, plus 10 ({@damage 3d6}) Piercing damage if the attack was made with {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}.

action.name: Longbow
entries: {@atkr r} {@hit 5}, range 150/600 ft. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d8 + 3}) Piercing damage, plus 10 ({@damage 3d6}) Piercing damage if the attack was made with {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}.

bonus.name: Aim
entries: Nicholas the Traveler has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the next attack roll he makes during the current turn.

reaction.name: Uncanny Dodge
entries: {@actTrigger} Nicholas the Traveler is hit by an attack roll. {@actResponse} Nicholas the Traveler halves the damage (round down) he takes from that attack.
Nicholas Sandford (born 1035 AE) is an Ascalonian traveler, a craftsman and a writer.

Nicholas met his dolyak friend, Professor Yakkington, during a snowstorm in the Northern Shiverpeaks. The two managed to survive by huddling together for warmth and they have been together ever since.

Due to the Searing, Nicholas lost his parents, friends and beloved Mary Malone. After having mourned in seclusion for years, his hair grown white, he started to travel the world and dropped his last name in favor of the moniker "the Traveler".
He has made many friends, including Gwen and Durmand, and is often seen collecting items to aid them (especially Durmand or Zinn) in some form, or catering to Yakkington's whims.

A true craftsman, Nicholas is able to make various tools and even small sculptures out of various items he finds. Once he helped Vael by making a disguise out of Quetzal Crests so Vael could spy on the Tengu of Cantha.
In exchange, he has gathered up a hefty supply of exotic and unique items to put in his Gifts of the Traveler, which he trades with adventurers in exchange for the items he needs.
Nicholas is very interested in local folklore, in fauna and flora. As a writer he has written a novel entitled "There with Yakkington: A Traveler's Tale.".

He also claims to be a gourmet chef, thanks to the various different foods he has tasted throughout his travels, which his friendship with Imperial Chef Yileng may suggest to be true, and a medic, as he is often seen trying to come up with some remedy to counteract various poisons (notably those inflicted by Dryders or Rot Wallows).

images.title: Nicholas the Traveler
images.altText: Nicholas the Traveler holding a Staff

trait.name: Charge
entries: If Professor Yakkington moves at least 20 feet straight toward a target and then hits it with a ram attack on the same turn, the target takes an extra 7 ({@damage 2d6}) damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a {@dc 13} Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}.

action.name: Ram
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}10 ({@damage 2d6 + 3}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Hooves
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one {@condition prone} creature. {@h}13 ({@damage 4d4 + 3}) bludgeoning damage.

Professor Yakkington (born in 1057 AE) is the companion of Nicholas Sandford the Traveler. He was found by Nicholas during a blizzard in Traveler's Vale, having been abandoned by his mother like most young Dolyaks perceived to be weak. After using each other for warmth in the blizzard, Yakkington has joined Nicholas in his travels across Tyria.

In their travels, Yakkington is the fighter of the pair, holding back the wildlife for Nick. He also has a good sense of smell which comes in handy when trying to find their path again should they get lost.

Since his travels, Yakkington has become pampered by Nicholas, and often becomes stubborn. He enjoys having daily sweets and eating Red Iris Flowers, but has a strong fear of snakes and darkness. Because of his fears, Nick often puts Enchanted Lodestones to act as lights at night when Yakkington sleeps. According to Nick, he is 6'8", weighs about a ton, and his lucky number is 5.

action.name: Summon Inferno (1/Day)
entries: {@actSave dex} {@dc 21}, each creature in a 60-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation}. {@i Failure:} 70 ({@damage 20d6}) Fire damage. {@i Success:} Half damage.

{@i Can only be used while the avatar is below 50% of its maximum {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. Resolves at the start of the avatar's next turn, during which it can't use any other actions and the area of effect is clearly visible.}

action.name: Summon Hounds (1/Day)
entries: The avatar magically summons up to two {@creature Hell Hound|XMM|Hell Hounds} to two points it can see within 60 feet of itself. The hounds take their turn immediately after the avatar on the same {@variantrule Initiative} count, and they obey the avatar. The hounds stay for 1 hour or until the die, the avatar dies, or they are more than 300 feet from the avatar.

entries: {@atk ms} {@hit 10} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}25 ({@damage 3d12 + 6}) fire damage.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The eternal casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 18}, +10 to hit with spell attacks).

trait.name: Incorporeal Movement
entries: The eternal can move through other creatures and objects as if they were {@quickref difficult terrain||3}. It takes 5 ({@damage 1d10}) force damage if it ends its turn inside an object.

trait.name: Ethereal Sight
entries: The eternal can see 60 feet into the Ethereal Plane when it is not on the Ethereal Plane.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The eternal uses either Mind Blade or Mind Expulsion, casts Channeling or Illusion of Haste, and casts Empathy and Shatter Enchantment.

action.name: Mind Expulsion
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 12} to hit, range 120 ft., one target. {@h}27 ({@damage 6d6 + 6}) psychic damage.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The eternal casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 20}, +12 to hit with spell attacks).

trait.name: Incorporeal Movement

trait.name: Ethereal Sight

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If the eternal fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

legendary.name: Attack
entries: The eternal uses Mind Blade or Mind Expulsion.

legendary.name: Move
entries: The eternal moves up to its speed without provoking opportunity attacks.

legendary.name: Spellcasting (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The eternal uses Spellcasting.

entries.name: Avatar of the Gods
entries: {@i Manifestations of Divinity}

Spiritual manifestations of the humans Gods of Tyria, the Avatars of the Gods take the form of whatever culture associates with that God. In the militaristic Ascalon, all Avatars clad in armor. In the artistic Echovald Forest the Avatars are more gentle in appearance. The Avatars appear in moments of great crisis or when a particular devout person prays to their god at one of their most holiest of sites. The Avatars not only represent the manifested will of the Gods, they are also liaisons and envoys, facilitating the communication between mortals and the Gods.
While not known to be particular combative, the Avatars are powerful creatures in their own right, embodying their gods domain in the way they fight. All Avatars have the following traits in common:
{@bold Divine Protection.} The avatar is immune to disease and cannot bet cursed.
{@bold Exalted Restoration.} If the avatar dies outside it's Gods plane, its body disappears, and it gains a new body instantly, reviving with all its Hit Points somewhere on that plane.
{@bold Magic Resistance.} The avatar has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

Being a manifestation of the God of War and Fire, the Avatar of Balthazar values combat prowess and valor above all else. It is typical for it to challenge supplicants to duels before even considering granting their requests.

entries.name: Eternals
entries: {@i Holy Servants of Balthazar}

Eternals are those souls blessed with eternal afterlife in Balthazar's realm, the Fissure of Woe, the eternal battlefield. It is honour given to those who achieve great things in life relating to war and battle. In the fissure they are tasked with fighting the endless battle against Balthazar's evil half brother Menzies, the lord of destruction.
All Eternals have a very high combat prowess from their previous live. All eternals have the following traits in common:
{@bold Ethereal Sight.} The eternal can see 60 feet into the Ethereal Plane when it is not on the Ethereal Plane.

{@bold Incorporeal Movement.} The eternal can move through other creatures and objects as if they were {@variantrule Difficult Terrain}. It takes 5 ({@damage 1d10}) Force damage if it ends its turn inside an object.

{@bold Etherealness.} The eternal casts the {@spell Etherealness} spell, requiring no spell components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability. The eternal is visible on the origin Plane while on the Border Ethereal and vice versa, but it can't affect or be affected by anything on the other plane.
Eternal Warriors are the most common type of eternal, as most professional soldiers and mercenaries end up in the Fissure of Woe.
Eternal Monks were once priests of Balthazar, now they use their divine blessing to heal their eternal kin.
Eternal Elementalist conjure the flames of their gods.
The Eternal Forgemaster is one of the oldest eternals in existence, it has forged the armaments of the gods.
Lord Khobay was once a high ranking eternal before he betrayed his god to work with Menzies.

entries.name: Shadow Army
entries: {@i Vile Servants of Menzies}

The Shadow Army are the forces used by Menzies, the evil half brother of Balthazar. They are primarily used in the conflict in the Fissure of Woe, where they enable Menzies conquest of his brother's realm.
The Shadow Army is supernaturally connected to each other, enabling powerful formations and battle tactics. All shadows have the following traits in common:
{@bold Devil's Sight.} The shadow can see normally in {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} and {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB}—both magical and nonmagical—within 120 feet of itself.
{@bold Living Shadow.} While in {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} or {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB}, the shadow has {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to damage that isn't Force, Psychic, or Radiant.
{@bold Sunlight Sensitivity.} While in sunlight, the shadow has Disadvantage on ability checks and attack rolls.
Shadow Warriors run into the fray, using their mastery with their shortsword and shield to slice through their enemies.
Shadow Rangers use their ability to create shadow duplicates of their projectiles to pepper their enemies with barrages of arrows.
Shadow Monks use their shadow weaving ability to restore and mend their allies.
Shadow Beasts wait patiently in the shadows to confound and punish their opponents, waiting for the perfect moment to strike.
Shadow Mesmers manipulate the feelings of their opponents to dull their blades and shatter their boons.
Masters of destructive fire magics, Shadow Elementals use their shadow weaving abilities to conjure powerful elemental blasts.
Abyssals are the most formidable solider of the Shadow Army, tearing through the enemy ranks using their large fists and earthshaking magic.

feat.name: Arcane Assassin
entries: You have gained the uncanny ability to extract arcane energy from your victims.

Once per {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}, you can choose expended spell slots to recover, when you land a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} with a weapon. The spell slots can have a combined level equal to your {@table Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}, and none of the slots can be level 6 or higher.

feat.name: Lunar Blessing
entries: Blessed by the Moon your {@subclassFeature Circle Forms|Druid|XPHB|Moon|XPHB|3} has improved even further.
{@b Challenge Rating.} The maximum {@variantrule Challenge Rating|XPHB} for the form equals your Druid level divided by 2 (round down).
{@b Armor Class.} Until you leave the form, your AC equals 15 plus your Wisdom modifier if that total is higher that the Beast's AC.
{@b Temporary Hit Points.} You gain a number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to four times you Druid level.

feat.name: Vengeance of the Protector
entries: Nothing infuriates you more than a friend in need.

While you are in combat, and another ally of yours is at 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} and making {@variantrule Death Saving Throw|XPHB|Death Saving Throws}, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on all {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB|D20 Tests}, as long you are aware of the ally's peril.

feat.name: Omen of Prophecy
entries: This feat replaces your {@subclassFeature Cosmic Omen|Druid|XPHB|Stars|XPHB|6} feature.
You have gained a more profound insight into the movement of the cosmos and can therefore influence the future more reliably.

Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can consult your Star Map for omens and make a Wisdom ({@skill Survival}) check. Until you finish your next {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you gain access to a special {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} based on whether you rolled an even or an odd number on the {@dice d20}. If you rolled a 20 on the {@dice d20} or a 30+ on your Wisdom ({@skill Survival}) check, you gain access to both (Weal and Woe) and the omen becomes stronger (see the values in parentheses):

Weal (even). Whenever a creature you can see within 30 (60) feet of you is about to make a {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB}, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to roll {@dice 1d6} ({@dice 2d6}) and add the number rolled to the total.

Woe (odd). Whenever a creature you can see within 30 (60) feet of you is about to make a {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB}, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to roll {@dice 1d6} ({@dice 2d6}) and subtract the number rolled from the total.

You can use this {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} a number of times equal to (twice) your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

feat.name: Warrior Initiate
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Warrior Points
entries: You have a number of Warrior Points equal to you {@table Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}. You regain all expended Warrior Points when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
You can expend Warrior Points to use certain Path of the Warrior benefits. This feat gives you one such benefit: Warrior Spells.

entries.name: Warrior Spells
entries: Choose a number of level 1 or lower Spells from the {@table Spells [Background: Warrior]|GW|Guild Wars}, {@table Spells [Background: Warrior]|GWP|Prophecies}, {@table Spells [Background: Warrior]|GWF|Factions}, {@table Spells [Background: Warrior]|GWN|Nightfall} or {@table Spells [Background: Warrior]|GWEotN|Eye of the North} tables equal to your {@table Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}. You always have them prepared.

Strength, Dexterity, or Constitution is your spellcasting ability for this feat's spells (choose when you select this feat). You can cast them without expending a spell slot by expending Warrior Points equal to the level of the spell. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have.

Whenever you gain a new level, you can replace one of the spells you chose for this feat with a different spell of the same level from the chosen spell list. When your {@table Proficiency Bonus|XPHB} increases choose additional spells until the number of prepared spells matches your {@table Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}.

feat.name: Warrior Adept
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Warrior Points Increase
entries: You gain additional Warrior Points equal to half your {@table Proficiency Bonus|XPHB} (round up).

entries.name: Advanced Warrior Spells
entries: You learn additional spells for your Warrior Spells feature. They use the same rules as the ones prepared by your Warrior Spells feature.

Choose a number of level 2 or lower Spells from the {@table Spells [Background: Warrior]|GW|Guild Wars}, {@table Spells [Background: Warrior]|GWP|Prophecies}, {@table Spells [Background: Warrior]|GWF|Factions}, {@table Spells [Background: Warrior]|GWN|Nightfall} or {@table Spells [Background: Warrior]|GWEotN|Eye of the North} tables equal to half your {@table Proficiency Bonus|XPHB} (round up). entries.name: Mesmer Spells
entries: Choose a number of level 1 or lower Spells from the {@table Spells [Background: Mesmer]|GW|Guild Wars}, {@table Spells [Background: Mesmer]|GWP|Prophecies}, {@table Spells [Background: Mesmer]|GWF|Factions}, {@table Spells [Background: Mesmer]|GWN|Nightfall} or {@table Spells [Background: Mesmer]|GWEotN|Eye of the North} tables equal to your {@table Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}. You always have them prepared.

Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this feat's spells (choose when you select this feat). You can cast them without expending a spell slot by expending Mesmer Points equal to the level of the spell. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have.

Whenever you gain a new level, you can replace one of the spells you chose for this feat with a different spell of the same level from the chosen spell list. When your {@table Proficiency Bonus|XPHB} increases choose additional spells until the number of prepared spells matches your {@table Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}.

feat.name: Mesmer Adept
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Mesmer Points Increase
entries: You gain additional Mesmer Points equal to half your {@table Proficiency Bonus|XPHB} (round up).

entries.name: Advanced Mesmer Spells
entries: You learn additional spells for your Mesmer Spells feature. They use the same rules as the ones prepared by your Mesmer Spells feature.

Choose a number of level 3 or lower Spells from the {@table Spells [Background: Mesmer]|GW|Guild Wars}, {@table Spells [Background: Mesmer]|GWP|Prophecies}, {@table Spells [Background: Mesmer]|GWF|Factions}, {@table Spells [Background: Mesmer]|GWN|Nightfall} or {@table Spells [Background: Mesmer]|GWEotN|Eye of the North} tables equal to half your {@table Proficiency Bonus|XPHB} (round up). You always have them prepared.

entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Mesmer Points Increase
entries: You gain additional Mesmer Points equal to half your {@table Proficiency Bonus|XPHB} (round up).

entries.name: Advanced Mesmer Spells
entries: You learn additional spells for your Mesmer Spells feature. They use the same rules as the ones prepared by your Mesmer Spells feature.

Choose a number of level 5 or lower Spells from the {@table Spells [Background: Mesmer]|GW|Guild Wars}, {@table Spells [Background: Mesmer]|GWP|Prophecies}, {@table Spells [Background: Mesmer]|GWF|Factions}, {@table Spells [Background: Mesmer]|GWN|Nightfall} or {@table Spells [Background: Mesmer]|GWEotN|Eye of the North} tables equal to half your {@table Proficiency Bonus|XPHB} (round up). You always have them prepared.

feat.name: Mesmer Exalted
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Mesmer Points Increase
entries: You gain additional Mesmer Points equal to half your {@table Proficiency Bonus|XPHB} (round up).

entries.name: Advanced Mesmer Spells
entries: You learn additional spells for your Mesmer Spells feature. They use the same rules as the ones prepared by your Mesmer Spells feature.

Choose a number of level 7 or lower Spells from the {@table Spells [Background: Mesmer]|GW|Guild Wars}, {@table Spells [Background: Mesmer]|GWP|Prophecies}, {@table Spells [Background: Mesmer]|GWF|Factions}, {@table Spells [Background: Mesmer]|GWN|Nightfall} or {@table Spells [Background: Mesmer]|GWEotN|Eye of the North} tables equal to half your {@table Proficiency Bonus|XPHB} (round up). You always have them prepared.

feat.name: Mesmer Ascendant
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Mesmer Points Increase
entries: You gain additional Mesmer Points equal to half your {@table Proficiency Bonus|XPHB} (round up).

entries.name: Advanced Mesmer Spells
entries: You learn additional spells for your Mesmer Spells feature. They use the same rules as the ones prepared by your Mesmer Spells feature.

Choose a number of level 9 or lower Spells from the {@table Spells [Background: Mesmer]|GW|Guild Wars}, {@table Spells [Background: Mesmer]|GWP|Prophecies}, {@table Spells [Background: Mesmer]|GWF|Factions}, {@table Spells [Background: Mesmer]|GWN|Nightfall} or {@table Spells [Background: Mesmer]|GWEotN|Eye of the North} tables equal to half your {@table Proficiency Bonus|XPHB} (round up). You always have them prepared.

feat.name: Elementalist Initiate
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Elementalist Points
entries: You have a number of Elementalist Points equal to you {@table Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}. You regain all expended Elementalist Points when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
You can expend Elementalist Points to use certain Path of the Elementalist benefits. This feat gives you one such benefit: Elementalist Spells.

entries.name: Elementalist Spells
entries: Choose a number of level 1 or lower Spells from the {@table Spells [Background: Elementalist]|GW|Guild Wars}, {@table Spells [Background: Elementalist]|GWP|Prophecies}, {@table Spells [Background: Elementalist]|GWF|Factions}, {@table Spells [Background: Elementalist]|GWN|Nightfall} or {@table Spells [Background: Elementalist]|GWEotN|Eye of the North} tables equal to your {@table Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}. You always have them prepared.

Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this feat's spells (choose when you select this feat). You can cast them without expending a spell slot by expending Elementalist Points equal to the level of the spell. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have.

Whenever you gain a new level, you can replace one of the spells you chose for this feat with a different spell of the same level from the chosen spell list. When your {@table Proficiency Bonus|XPHB} increases choose additional spells until the number of prepared spells matches your {@table Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}.

feat.name: Elementalist Adept
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Elementalist Points Increase
entries: You gain additional Elementalist Points equal to half your {@table Proficiency Bonus|XPHB} (round up).

entries.name: Advanced Elementalist Spells
entries: You learn additional spells for your Elementalist Spells feature. They use the same rules as the ones prepared by your Elementalist Spells feature.

Choose a number of level 3 or lower Spells from the {@table Spells [Background: Elementalist]|GW|Guild Wars}, {@table Spells [Background: Elementalist]|GWP|Prophecies}, {@table Spells [Background: Elementalist]|GWF|Factions}, {@table Spells [Background: Elementalist]|GWN|Nightfall} or {@table Spells [Background: Elementalist]|GWEotN|Eye of the North} tables equal to half your {@table Proficiency Bonus|XPHB} (round up). You always have them prepared.

entries: You gain additional Elementalist Points equal to half your {@table Proficiency Bonus|XPHB} (round up).

entries: You learn additional spells for your Elementalist Spells feature. They use the same rules as the ones prepared by your Elementalist Spells feature.

Choose a number of level 5 or lower Spells from the {@table Spells [Background: Elementalist]|GW|Guild Wars}, {@table Spells [Background: Elementalist]|GWP|Prophecies}, {@table Spells [Background: Elementalist]|GWF|Factions}, {@table Spells [Background: Elementalist]|GWN|Nightfall} or {@table Spells [Background: Elementalist]|GWEotN|Eye of the North} tables equal to half your {@table Proficiency Bonus|XPHB} (round up). You always have them prepared.

entries: You gain additional Elementalist Points equal to half your {@table Proficiency Bonus|XPHB} (round up).

entries.name: Advanced Elementalist Spells
entries: You learn additional spells for your Elementalist Spells feature. They use the same rules as the ones prepared by your Elementalist Spells feature.

Choose a number of level 7 or lower Spells from the {@table Spells [Background: Elementalist]|GW|Guild Wars}, {@table Spells [Background: Elementalist]|GWP|Prophecies}, {@table Spells [Background: Elementalist]|GWF|Factions}, {@table Spells [Background: Elementalist]|GWN|Nightfall} or {@table Spells [Background: Elementalist]|GWEotN|Eye of the North} tables equal to half your {@table Proficiency Bonus|XPHB} (round up). You always have them prepared.

entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Elementalist Points Increase
entries: You gain additional Elementalist Points equal to half your {@table Proficiency Bonus|XPHB} (round up).

entries.name: Advanced Elementalist Spells
entries: You learn additional spells for your Elementalist Spells feature. They use the same rules as the ones prepared by your Elementalist Spells feature.

Choose a number of level 9 or lower Spells from the {@table Spells [Background: Elementalist]|GW|Guild Wars}, {@table Spells [Background: Elementalist]|GWP|Prophecies}, {@table Spells [Background: Elementalist]|GWF|Factions}, {@table Spells [Background: Elementalist]|GWN|Nightfall} or {@table Spells [Background: Elementalist]|GWEotN|Eye of the North} tables equal to half your {@table Proficiency Bonus|XPHB} (round up). You always have them prepared.

spell.name: Gash

time.condition: which you take immediately before hitting a target with a Melee weapon or an Unarmed Strike

entries: {@variantrule Adrenaline|GW|Adrenaline:} 6, {@variantrule Weapon Attack Spell|GW}
You wound your enemy grievously.

On a hit, the target suffers the weapon attack's normal effects, takes {@damage 1d6} damage, and, if the target is {@condition bleeding|GW}, the target gains a {@condition deep wound|GW} for the duration. On a successful save, the target is affected for 1 round only.
At the end of each of its turns, the target can make a Constitution saving throw. On a success, the condition ends.
Gash
Sword Attack. If this attack hits a Bleeding foe, you strike for 5...17...20 more damage and that foe suffers a Deep Wound, lowering that foe's maximum Health by 20% for 5...17...20 seconds.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The target also takes {@damage 1d6} damage for each slot level above 1 on a hit.

spell.name: Wild Blow

time.condition: which you take immediately before hitting a target with a Melee weapon or an Unarmed Strike

entries: {@variantrule Unblockable|GW}, {@variantrule Weapon Attack Spell|GW}
You imbue your weapon with as much magical power as possible, striking with all your might.
On a hit, the target suffers the weapon attack's critical effects and must make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, any {@variantrule stance|GW} on the target ends.
You lose all adrenaline.
Wild Blow
Melee Attack. Lose all adrenaline. If it hits, this attack will result in a critical hit and any stance being used by your target ends. This attack cannot be blocked.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The target also takes {@damage 1d8} damage for each slot level above 3 on a hit.

spell.name: Flare
entries: Send out a searing hot flare.
Choose one creature that you can see within range. Make a ranged spell attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 4d6} fire damage.

{@variantrule Overcast|GW|Overcast:} On a hit, each creature other than the target in a 10-foot-radius sphere centered on the target, must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes {@damage 4d6} fire damage. On a successful save, a target takes half as much damage.
Flare
Spell. Send out a flare that strikes target foe for 20...56...65 fire damage if it hits. If you are Overcast, Flare hits adjacent foes as well.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage each increases by {@dice 1d6} for each slot level above 1.

spell.name: Frenzy
entries: {@variantrule Stance|GW}
Enter a frenzy, disregarding any defense.
